buggy_code:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_csv('features.csv', parse_dates=['date'], index_col='date')

# Fill NaN with column MEAN — uses full series including future values
df = df.fillna(df.mean())

# Use as model features
X = df.values
clean_code:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_csv('features.csv', parse_dates=['date'], index_col='date')

# Fill NaN with EXPANDING mean — only uses past values at each point
df = df.apply(lambda col: col.fillna(col.expanding().mean()))

# Use as model features
X = df.values
description: NaN values filled with column mean computed over the entire series including future data.
explanation: df.mean() computes the mean of each column using all observations, including future ones. A NaN value at time T is filled with a mean that includes data from T+1, T+2, etc. This injects future information into every row that had a missing value, creating subtle lookahead bias that is very hard to detect.
suggested_fix: Use expanding().mean() to fill NaN with only historically available mean values.

issues.title: fillna with column mean uses future data for imputation
fillna with column mean uses future data for imputation
